# Allison (Allie) Krier — Personal Landing Page

*Project title. Update if the repo name changes.*

## Description

*2–4 sentences describing the project. Update if the purpose shifts.*

A personal landing page built to help Allison (Allie) Krier stand out to retail-industry recruiters beyond what a traditional resume can offer. The page showcases her skills, projects, and professional experience as a Business Analytics and Information Systems student at the University of Iowa, graduating May 2027. It is designed for hiring managers at mid-sized retail companies in the Midwest who need to quickly verify her data analysis capabilities and professional background.

## Features

*Update this list whenever a new feature is added or removed.*

- Professional hero section with circular headshot, name, tagline, and call-to-action buttons
- Skills section with grouped, pill-badge tags — Data & Analytics vs. Business & Productivity
- Project cards for Titanic Data Analysis, Target Merchandising Case Competition, and banking operations experience
- Work experience section with role, organization, date, and bullet-point detail
- Contact section with icon-linked email, LinkedIn, and GitHub badges
- Fully responsive layout — single column on mobile, two-column project grid on desktop
- Sticky navigation bar with smooth-scroll anchor links to every section
- WCAG 2.2 Level AA accessibility: descriptive alt text, logical heading hierarchy, keyboard-navigable links

## Tech Stack

*Update the table if any tools are added or swapped out.*

| Technology | Purpose |
|---|---|
| HTML5 | Page structure and semantic markup |
| CSS3 | All styling, layout, and responsive design |
| Google Fonts (Inter) | Typography — loaded via CDN |
| shields.io | README badges |
| Python `http.server` | Local development server (Replit) |

## Getting Started

### Prerequisites

*List anything that must be installed before cloning. Update if deployment method changes.*

- A modern web browser (Chrome, Safari, or Firefox)
- No build tools, package managers, or runtimes required — this is a pure static site

### Installation

*Update the repo URL once the final GitHub repo is confirmed.*

1. Clone the repository:
   ```bash
   git clone https://github.com/allkrier/3300-landing-page-2.git
   ```
2. Navigate into the project folder:
   ```bash
   cd 3300-landing-page-2
   ```
3. Open `index.html` directly in your browser — no build step needed:
   ```bash
   open index.html
   ```
   Or, to serve it locally with Python:
   ```bash
   python3 -m http.server 5000
   ```
   Then visit `http://localhost:5000` in your browser.

## Usage

*Update this section if new pages or configuration options are added.*

Open `index.html` in any browser to view the full landing page. Use the sticky navigation bar at the top to jump to any section: About, Skills, Projects, Experience, or Contact.

To customize the content, edit `index.html` directly — all text, links, and image paths are in plain HTML. To change colors, fonts, or layout, edit `css/stylesheet.css`. All design tokens (colors, spacing, font sizes) are defined as CSS custom properties at the top of that file under `:root` for easy updates.

To swap in a new headshot, replace `images/allkrier_headshot.png` with the new file and keep the same filename, or update the `src` attribute in the `<img>` tag inside the hero section.

## Project Structure

*Update the tree whenever files are added, moved, or removed.*

```
3300-landing-page-2/
├── index.html              # Main HTML file — all page content and structure
├── css/
│   └── stylesheet.css      # All styles — layout, colors, typography, responsive rules
├── images/
│   └── allkrier_headshot.png  # Professional headshot used in the hero section
├── js/
│   └── scripts.js          # Reserved for future JavaScript (currently empty)
├── PRD.md                  # Product Requirement Document — goals, audience, content spec
├── STANDARDS.md            # Technical and design standards for this project
└── README.md               # This file
```

## Changelog

*Add a new entry at the top of this section for every release.*

### v1.0.0 — 2026-03-25

**Initial release**

- Hero section with circular headshot, full name, tagline, and CTA buttons
- About Me section with bio, academic credentials, and extracurricular context
- Skills section with two grouped categories and pill-badge tags
- Projects section with three cards: Titanic Data Analysis, Target Merchandising Case Competition, and Port Washington State Bank operations
- Work experience section with Port Washington State Bank and NRF Student Association
- Contact section with email, LinkedIn, and GitHub icon badges
- Sticky responsive navigation with smooth-scroll anchor links
- Fully responsive layout from 320px to desktop
- WCAG 2.2 Level AA accessibility compliance

## Known Issues / To-Do

*Check off items as they are completed. Add new items as they come up.*

- [ ] Add a more detailed project description for the Titanic Data Analysis (link to GitHub repo or PDF once available)
- [ ] Add a detailed description and outcome for the Target Merchandising Case Competition once more information is available
- [ ] Add a `LICENSE` file to the repository root (select MIT License template on GitHub)
- [ ] Test rendering on Safari iOS and Chrome Android at 375px viewport
- [ ] Confirm all shield.io badge URLs render correctly after the repo is public

## Roadmap

*Update this section each semester or when priorities shift.*

- Add a dedicated Projects detail view (modal or separate section) with screenshots, methodology, and key findings for each project
- Integrate a downloadable resume button (PDF) once permitted by course guidelines
- Add a "Currently Learning" or "Coursework" section to highlight relevant BAIS classes
- Refine the hero tagline once a specific target role and industry specialization solidifies
- Add site analytics (e.g., simple privacy-friendly counter) to track recruiter visits against PRD success metrics

## Contributing

*This is a personal project. Update this section if you open it to collaborators.*

This repository is primarily a personal portfolio project, but feedback and suggestions are welcome. To propose a change:

1. Fork the repository on GitHub
2. Create a new branch for your change:
   ```bash
   git checkout -b suggestion/your-idea
   ```
3. Make your edits to `index.html` or `css/stylesheet.css`
4. Commit your changes with a clear message:
   ```bash
   git commit -m "Suggest: brief description of change"
   ```
5. Push to your fork and open a pull request with a description of what you changed and why

## Acknowledgements

*Update this list whenever a new tool or resource is used.*

- [Google Fonts — Inter](https://fonts.google.com/specimen/Inter) — typography loaded via CDN
- [shields.io](https://shields.io) — README badge generation
- [Lucide Icons](https://lucide.dev) — SVG icon source reference for contact badges
- [MDN Web Docs](https://developer.mozilla.org) — HTML5 and CSS3 reference documentation
- [WebAIM Contrast Checker](https://webaim.org/resources/contrastchecker/) — WCAG 2.2 color contrast verification
- Initial page structure and stylesheet generated with [Claude](https://claude.ai) (Anthropic) via Replit, reviewed and approved by the author
- Icon: [vibe coding](https://thenounproject.com) by iqonica from [Noun Project](https://thenounproject.com) (CC BY 3.0)
